mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for equity cash flows, systematically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, with Rank IC > 0.05 contradicting the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer and investment demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint decline of HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with a positive mean return observed in > 50% of co-occurring decline months.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day returns during expansion regimes, yielding a negative Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates above 3.0 percent annually, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening and eroded real consumer purchasing power, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.0 percent annual threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 60-day returns in > 50% of occurrences, invalidating the positive earnings transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly GDP growth reflects sustained aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, which systematically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02 across all market regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during late-cycle expansions signal weakening consumer demand and impending earnings revisions, which trigger sharp negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS changes fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with positive returns occurring in > 55% of decline months during late-cycle phases.
